title: Strings

contentBlocks.text: Text in Python is called a string. You can join two strings together with a + sign — this is called concatenation.
Create a variable named greeting that combines "Hello, " with your own name, then print it. For example: Hello, Sam
Remember the space! "Hello, " + "Sam" gives Hello, Sam — but "Hello," + "Sam" gives Hello,Sam with no space.

starterCode:
# Build a greeting by joining strings together
greeting = "Hello, " + "____"
print(greeting)

testCases.failureMessage: Your printed output should start with "Hello, " followed by a name.

hints: Use the + operator to join two strings: "Hello, " + "Sam"
Store the result in a variable called greeting, then print(greeting).
